S.t: Text-to-image generation to accelerate sustainable fashion design research
S.inst: Belfast School of Art, Ulster University
S.s: Text-to-image tools (Midjourney, Patterned AI, Stable Diffusion XL) speed up design iteration and trend prediction within a sustainability-focused fashion design research project, aimed at reducing material waste from physical prototyping.

S.t: Vector embeddings and an LLM to rapidly classify combined legal-linguistic datasets
S.inst: School of Communication and Media, Ulster University
S.s: Vector embeddings paired with a generative LLM classify and retrieve relevant material across a combined legal and linguistic dataset, used at the data-retrieval/thematic-analysis stage of a law-and-language research project.

S.t: AI co-scientist proposes drug-repurposing hypotheses for liver fibrosis, validated in human liver organoids
S.s: Google's multi-agent "AI co-scientist" system (built on Gemini 2.0) was tasked with proposing epigenomic-modifier drug candidates with anti-fibrotic activity; researchers experimentally tested its top proposals in human liver organoids and validated two of three, including repurposing the already-approved drug vorinostat — AI used at the hypothesis-generation stage, with the biology itself confirmed by wet-lab experiment.

S.t: AI co-scientist independently proposes the mechanism behind mobile bacterial gene-transfer islands
S.inst: Imperial College London, with Google collaborators
S.s: Tasked only with explaining how mobile genetic islands (cf-PICIs) spread across different bacterial species, Google's AI co-scientist independently proposed the "tail piracy" mechanism the human research team had spent years establishing experimentally — reaching the same hypothesis in days, which the team then confirmed in the lab.

S.t: Using Gemini to generate initial themes from family-planning-clinic interview transcripts
S.by: Claire Kelley, Deja Logan, Bonnie Solomon
S.s: Google Gemini 2.5 Pro generated inductive initial themes from real Title X family-planning-clinic interview transcripts on reproductive health services; researchers then refined those themes and applied them deductively across the dataset, concluding AI should augment rather than replace human qualitative coding.

S.t: AI-generated initial codes for a cross-EU qualitative study on cancer-prevention literacy
S.by: Barrera, Feliu, Espina, Brand, Zeeb, Ahmed
S.s: ATLAS.ti's NLP-driven coding feature generated over 5,000 initial codes from interview data collected across multiple EU case studies on cancer-prevention health literacy; researchers then consolidated and refined the AI-generated codes into the study's final thematic framework.

S.t: Topic modeling a Civil War-era Richmond newspaper to surface themes invisible to manual reading
S.inst: Digital Scholarship Lab, University of Richmond
S.s: LDA-based topic modeling of roughly 1,400 issues of the Richmond Dispatch (1860–65) surfaced recurring themes and shifts in Confederate Richmond's social and political life that weren't visible through manual reading of the archive, feeding into a peer-reviewed Journal of American History article. Note: this predates the generative-AI wave (machine-learning topic modeling, not an LLM) — worth a scope call on whether it fits a GenAI-focused catalog.

S.t: A custom neural network re-dates Dead Sea Scroll fragments from handwriting analysis
S.s: "Enoch", a neural network trained on ink-trace and letterform patterns from radiocarbon-dated fragments, predicted manuscript dates (within about ±30 years) for undated Dead Sea Scroll fragments, agreeing with paleographic estimates 79% of the time and suggesting some scrolls are older than previously thought — part of the ERC-funded "Hands That Wrote the Bible" project. Note: a classification neural net, not a generative model — same scope caveat as the topic-modeling entry above.

S.t: GrantCheck: hybrid NLP/LLM tool flags non-compliant language in real grant applications
S.by: Shi, Oztekin, Matthew, Bortle, et al.
S.s: A hybrid rule-based NLP and LLM tool (via Amazon Bedrock) flags language in draft grant proposals that's misaligned with current NIH/federal policy requirements and suggests compliant alternative phrasing, deployed to help researchers actually writing federal grant applications — an assistive/compliance tool rather than a source of intellectual content.

S.t: Using ChatGPT and Claude as co-developers to build a behavioral-research data pipeline
S.s: Researchers used ChatGPT and Claude as interactive co-developers to build a Docker/n8n ETL pipeline converting FaceReader video-analysis output from a 102-participant Cyberball social-exclusion study into analysis-ready datasets, without needing advanced coding or DevOps skills themselves — AI used at the data-cleaning/pipeline-engineering stage of a real behavioral study.

S.t: Replacing a 6-month outsourced vendor process with AI transcription and translation of qualitative interviews
S.inst: Nuffield Department of Women's & Reproductive Health, University of Oxford
S.s: A DPhil candidate replaced a roughly six-month outsourced vendor process for transcribing and translating (primarily Hindi-dialect) qualitative interview audio with Gemini and NotebookLM, cutting turnaround to hours while cross-checking AI output against vendor transcripts for accuracy.
S.q: Instead of transcription and translation taking months, it now takes hours.

S.t: Using OpenAI Codex to re-OCR and structurally analyze medieval manuscript catalogues
S.inst: Regent's Park College & Kellogg College, University of Oxford
S.s: A medievalist and computer scientist used OpenAI's Codex coding assistant and GPT-5.x models to write Python programs that re-OCR poorly scanned manuscript summary catalogues, infer structural fields like genre and manuscript type, and export the results to Excel and TEI-XML for further scholarly analysis.
S.q: What I can imagine, I can build at speed.

S.t: Using ChatGPT Edu's Deep Research to import cross-industry methods into surgical quality-assurance research
S.inst: Nuffield Department of Surgical Sciences, University of Oxford
S.s: A surgeon-researcher used ChatGPT Edu's 'Deep Research' function to search literature from unrelated high-stakes industries — aerospace, advanced manufacturing — for quality-assurance methodologies applicable to intraoperative surgical safety, replacing what would previously have required weeks of speculative searching.
S.q: Treat ChatGPT not as a search engine, but as a tireless interdisciplinary research assistant.

S.t: ChatGPT Edu for debugging, architecture ideation, and manuscript refinement in a protein-structure prediction project
S.inst: Oxford-GSK Institute of Molecular and Computational Medicine, University of Oxford
S.s: A machine learning researcher used ChatGPT Edu to debug PyTorch code, suggest neural-network architecture changes, and refine manuscript writing while developing a protein-structure-prediction explainability prototype, completing a working prototype in about a week.
S.q: Treat ChatGPT as a collaborator, not a search engine. Provide context, test assumptions, and validate results.

S.t: Using Gemini Deep Research to cut a week-long literature survey down to a day
S.inst: Faculty of Theology and Religion, University of Oxford
S.s: A research fellow used Gemini's Deep Research function to conduct literature surveys that typically took a week in about a day, and separately used Gemini to rapidly prototype a web-based virtual viva-voce examiner tool.
S.q: It turned a week's work into about a day's work, but it's important to note that as well as saving time it produced better results than I think I could have achieved by myself.

S.t: Training a machine-learning model to classify a century of South Carolina statutes as Jim Crow law
S.by: K. Boyd, V. Srivastava, C. Frear, L. DuPre, N. Gupta, B. Donaldson
S.inst: University of South Carolina Libraries, in partnership with UNC-Chapel Hill Libraries
S.s: Extending UNC-Chapel Hill's original 'On the Books: Jim Crow and Algorithms of Resistance' project via a Mellon Foundation-funded sub-award, a University of South Carolina Libraries team trained a machine-learning text classifier to sort roughly a century of South Carolina legislative acts (1868–1968) into Jim Crow and non-Jim Crow law, surfacing discriminatory statutes that would be impractical to find through manual archival review alone.

S.t: Using ChatGPT to build a low-cost automated data pipeline for a dermatology residency study
S.by: Dr Naveen Manohar, Dr Shruthi S. Prasad
S.inst: The Oxford Medical College, Bangalore & Indira Medical College and Hospitals, Pandur
S.s: Two dermatology residency programs in India built a free AI-assisted data-collection workflow — Google Forms feeding into ChatGPT-generated automation scripts — to auto-code survey responses, calculate standardized quality-of-life scores (DLQI, DASS-21), generate PDF case report forms, and send instant notifications, cutting data-entry time by 69% and errors by 72% compared to the prior paper-based process.

S.t: Switching a take-home midterm to an in-person final cut the class average from 96% to 48.6%
S.s: After an unproctored take-home midterm in a welfare economics/social choice theory course produced a 96% class average (versus a historical 65–80%) with 40 perfect scores and answers bearing hallmarks of AI generation, the instructor switched the final exam to an in-person, proctored format.
S.ev: The in-person final's average fell to 48.6% — the course's historic low — with 3 students scoring zero. Of the 27 students who subsequently dropped the course or skipped the final, 22 had scored perfectly on the take-home midterm, a strong proxy signal that the drop-off was concentrated among students who could no longer rely on unproctored AI assistance.
S.q: This exam was harder than the exams I wrote in the past...take-home is an opportunity to challenge the class a little bit more.

S.t: Using AI to clean and explore two decades of citizen-science bird-sighting data
S.inst: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries (Hopkins AI Lab)
S.s: A Hopkins AI Lab consultation helped an ornithology researcher develop a strategy to clean and explore a bird-sighting dataset built up over roughly two decades from citizen-science websites, automating detection of missing values, errors, and duplicates in a dataset too large to check row by row.

S.t: Mapping pothole locations from street imagery with a pretrained pavement-crack-detection model
S.inst: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries (Hopkins AI Lab)
S.s: Using Esri's ArcGIS Pavement Crack Detection pretrained model, a Hopkins AI Lab consultation helped map road-damage locations across Baltimore City to help prioritize municipal repair crews' work.

S.t: Quantifying hurricane building damage from before/after satellite imagery
S.inst: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries (Hopkins AI Lab)
S.s: A Hopkins AI Lab consultation applied ArcGIS's Building Change Detection pretrained model to before/after satellite imagery of a hurricane-affected area, automatically locating buildings that were damaged or destroyed rather than assessing the imagery by eye.

S.t: Designing a geographically-referenced neighborhood safety survey with ArcGIS Survey123 Assistant
S.inst: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries (Hopkins AI Lab)
S.s: A Hopkins AI Lab consultation used the ArcGIS Survey123 Assistant to design a geographically-referenced public-perception survey on neighborhood safety, distributed to community groups as an alternative to door-to-door data collection.

S.t: Debugging a brain-tumor-detection ML model's Python code
S.inst: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries (Hopkins AI Lab)
S.s: A Hopkins AI Lab consultation helped a researcher developing a machine-learning model for early brain-tumor detection from MRI scans debug Python code that was producing inaccurate predictions, with AI interpreting the errors and suggesting fixes.

S.t: Using computational text analysis to detect reader address at scale across 3,000 novels
S.inst: AI Humanities Lab, Washington University in St. Louis
S.s: The 'Keeping You Close' project uses a computational method that separates dialogue from narration to detect direct reader address at scale across a corpus of more than 3,000 nineteenth- and twentieth-century Anglophone novels, combining that distant-reading approach with close reading to study a literary-historical question about how novels address their readers. Published in College Literature.

S.t: Using machine learning to study how character descriptions shape literary realism
S.inst: AI Humanities Lab, Washington University in St. Louis
S.s: The 'Reading Minds' project uses machine learning to analyze how descriptions of characters' social behavior in literary texts engage readers' real-life social cognition, combining literary studies, narratology, computer science, and cognitive-science theory to build a new account of character realism. Forthcoming in Poetics Today and Digital Scholarship in the Humanities.

S.t: Using ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and PaperQA as co-pilots for a protein-network analysis of invasive cancer
S.by: Hongda Jiang, Jahziel Chase, Lianxiang Fu, Sathvik Shivaram, Anton V. Sinitskiy
S.s: Researchers used ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and PaperQA to automate parts of a real biotech workflow — literature review, code generation, and functional network analysis of public proteomic data — for a study of protein networks in invasive colorectal cancer, identifying cytoskeleton- and signaling-related networks consistent with known biology. A separate attempt to run the same analysis fully autonomously via an AI agent framework failed from hallucinated data, goal misinterpretation, and instability, illustrating that the tools assisted rather than replaced the human researchers.

S.t: Using computational distributional semantics to track conceptual change across 60,000 Early Modern English texts
S.by: Susan Fitzmaurice, Justyna A. Robinson, Marc Alexander, Iona C. Hine, Seth Mehl, Fraser Dallachy
S.inst: Universities of Sheffield, Glasgow, and Sussex
S.s: The AHRC-funded 'Linguistic DNA' project applied computational distributional-semantics methods to roughly 60,000 Early Modern English printed texts (EEBO-TCP, later extended with ECCO) to discover 'discursive concepts' — clusters of co-occurring expressions — and trace how they shifted between about 1500 and 1800, aiming to surface relationships between words and ideas that exceed human intuition through big-data computational analysis.

S.t: Building a secure, private speech-transcription pipeline with Whisper and GitHub Copilot
S.inst: Department of Education, University of Oxford
S.s: A researcher used GitHub Copilot to build a secure, GDPR-compliant, HPC-compatible speech-transcription pipeline around OpenAI's open-source Whisper models, replacing manual interview transcription -- previously hours of typing per hour of audio -- with automated processing in minutes, enabling large-scale qualitative analysis of sensitive audio data that couldn't previously be sent to cloud-based transcription tools.
S.q: Copilot, not autopilot: review and revise the output conscientiously and relentlessly.

newcastle.blurb: Case studies from Newcastle University Business School's teaching-practice microsite.

ucl.label: University College London — Generative AI Case Studies
ucl.blurb: Individual case studies from UCL's teaching & learning case-study hub, plus one from the UCL Education Conference blog; the collection's own filtered list renders client-side, so entries here were found via direct links to the underlying pages.

stanfordaimes.label: Stanford University — AIMES Library of Teaching Examples
stanfordaimes.blurb: Faculty-submitted teaching artifacts from real Stanford courses using generative AI, via the Center for Teaching and Learning's AIMES library — distinct from the Stanford Accelerator for Learning repository used only as a UI model for this catalog.

michigan.label: University of Michigan — GenAI Use Cases
michigan.blurb: Teaching-specific entries from U-M's public generative AI use-case collection (admin/clinical-operations-only entries excluded).

cornell.label: Cornell University — Center for Teaching Innovation Case Study Library
cornell.blurb: Faculty-documented case studies of real classroom trials from Cornell's Center for Teaching Innovation.

gatech.label: Georgia Tech — Center for Teaching and Learning Project Showcase
gatech.blurb: Faculty projects from Georgia Tech's Center for Teaching and Learning showcase.

duke.label: Duke University — Center for Teaching and Learning Portraits of Practice
duke.blurb: Faculty portraits of practice from Duke's Center for Teaching and Learning.

asu.label: Arizona State University — Case Study
asu.blurb: A case study from ASU's technology news coverage of classroom AI use.

purdue.label: Purdue University — Innovation Hub
purdue.blurb: A case study from Purdue's Provost Innovation Hub.

harvard.label: Harvard Business School — Faculty Case Study
harvard.blurb: A faculty-written case study from Harvard Business School.

vanderbilt.label: Vanderbilt University — Case Study
vanderbilt.blurb: A widely-reported classroom experiment from a Vanderbilt computer science course.

unsw.label: UNSW Sydney — AI Case Study News Series
unsw.blurb: A news series of named, concrete case studies from UNSW's Portfolio of Education.

uts.label: University of Technology Sydney — AI in Assessments Case Studies
uts.blurb: Four named academics' case studies from a single UTS Education Express blog post.

adelaide.label: University of Adelaide — HERDSA Connect Case Study
adelaide.blurb: A practitioner case study published via HERDSA Connect.

macquarie.label: Macquarie University — Virtual Peer AI Chatbot Pilot
macquarie.blurb: A Microsoft-covered pilot of an AI chatbot in a Macquarie University psychology unit.

auckland.label: University of Auckland — TeachWell Case Studies in Teaching
auckland.blurb: Case studies from the University of Auckland's TeachWell Digital practice-sharing hub.

ubc.label: University of British Columbia Okanagan — Centre for Teaching and Learning
ubc.blurb: A practitioner case study from UBC Okanagan's Centre for Teaching and Learning.

toronto.label: University of Toronto — Teaching with Generative AI Example Library
toronto.blurb: Named instructor examples from U of T's Centre for Teaching Support & Innovation.

curtin.label: Curtin University — Assessment Design Studio Case Studies
curtin.blurb: Individual case studies from Curtin's Assessment 2030 Design Studio; most of the studio's 26 case studies don't involve generative AI — only the ones that do are catalogued here.

mdpieducsci.label: MDPI Education Sciences — Live Case Study Paper
mdpieducsci.blurb: A peer-reviewed journal paper describing a live-case-study teaching trial.

ascilite2025.label: ASCILITE 2025 Conference Proceedings
ascilite2025.blurb: Peer-reviewed conference papers describing real classroom or institutional trials of generative AI, screened the same way as the AdaptEdu 2024 proceedings — proposals and work-in-progress reports excluded.

ascilite2024.label: ASCILITE 2024 Conference Proceedings
ascilite2024.blurb: Peer-reviewed conference papers describing real classroom trials, including four individually reported case studies from one Arts Faculty paper — screened the same way as the AdaptEdu 2024 proceedings.

unisa-ioa.label: University of South Australia — Interactive Oral Assessment
unisa-ioa.blurb: Press coverage and instructor interviews on UniSA's switch from written exams to live one-on-one oral assessment in health-science courses.

ioa-multisubject.label: Interactive Oral Assessment — 25-Subject Study (Teaching in Higher Education)
ioa-multisubject.blurb: A peer-reviewed, three-year study of Interactive Oral Assessment rolled out across 25 subjects at an Australian university.

buv-aias.label: British University Vietnam — AI Assessment Scale (AIAS) Pilot
buv-aias.blurb: A peer-reviewed, institution-wide pilot of the AI Assessment Scale across roughly 2,500 students, published in the Australasian Journal of Educational Technology (preprint: arxiv.org/abs/2403.14692).

ulster-adaptiveai.label: Ulster University — AdaptiveAI Case Studies
ulster-adaptiveai.blurb: Researcher-submitted examples of AI used within real Ulster University research projects, curated by the AdaptiveAI project alongside its 'Start AI Talks' seminar series (blogs.ulster.ac.uk/adaptiveai/start-ai-talks/).

google-aicoscientist.label: Google AI Co-Scientist — Independent Research Collaborations
google-aicoscientist.blurb: Peer-reviewed studies in which Google's multi-agent 'AI co-scientist' system generated real scientific hypotheses, later validated experimentally by the human research teams.

childtrends-qualcoding.label: Child Trends — AI-Assisted Qualitative Interview Analysis
childtrends-qualcoding.blurb: A published case study on using Google Gemini to support thematic coding of real reproductive-health interview transcripts.

sage-cancerliteracy.label: SAGE — International Journal of Qualitative Methods
sage-cancerliteracy.blurb: A peer-reviewed study using ATLAS.ti's AI coding feature within a cross-EU qualitative study on cancer-prevention health literacy.

richmond-dispatch.label: University of Richmond — Mining the Dispatch
richmond-dispatch.blurb: A Digital Scholarship Lab project applying topic modeling to a Civil War-era Richmond newspaper archive, published in the Journal of American History.

deadseascrolls-enoch.label: University of Groningen — Dating the Dead Sea Scrolls with AI
deadseascrolls-enoch.blurb: The ERC-funded 'Hands That Wrote the Bible' project's use of a custom neural network to re-date Dead Sea Scroll fragments from handwriting analysis, published in PLOS One.

grantcheck-umass.label: UMass Chan Medical School — GrantCheck
grantcheck-umass.blurb: A JMIR Formative Research paper on a hybrid NLP/LLM tool assisting researchers with real federal grant-compliance drafting.

cyberball-etl.label: Automating a Behavioral-Research Data Pipeline (Cyberball Paradigm)
cyberball-etl.blurb: An MDPI Informatics paper on using LLMs as co-developers to build a data pipeline for a real 102-participant social-psychology study.

oxford-airesearch.label: University of Oxford AI Competency Centre — AI in Research Case Studies
oxford-airesearch.blurb: An ongoing, filterable collection of named-researcher case studies on using generative AI within the research process itself, run by Oxford's AI Competency Centre — distinct from the broader 'oxnarr' staff-use collection.

onthebooks-jimcrow.label: On the Books: Jim Crow and Algorithms of Resistance (UNC / USC Libraries)
onthebooks-jimcrow.blurb: A Mellon Foundation-funded UNC-Chapel Hill project, extended via a University of South Carolina Libraries sub-award, that trained a machine-learning model to classify historical state legislative acts as Jim Crow or non-Jim Crow law.

cureus-dermpilot.label: Cureus — AI-Assisted Digital Workflow for Dermatology Residency Research
cureus-dermpilot.blurb: A peer-reviewed pilot report from two Indian medical colleges on using ChatGPT to automate data coding, scoring, and reporting for a real clinical observational study.

brown-serrano.label: Brown University — Economics Exam Format Switch
brown-serrano.blurb: Inside Higher Ed coverage of a Brown University economics professor's switch from a take-home to an in-person final exam after suspected widespread AI use, with before/after score data.

jhu-ailab.label: Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries — Hopkins AI Lab Case Studies
jhu-ailab.blurb: Short, researcher-anonymized case studies from Johns Hopkins Sheridan Libraries' Hopkins AI Lab, describing real consultations on using AI tools within an ongoing research project — only the institution is named, not the individual researcher.

washu-aihumanities.label: Washington University in St. Louis — AI Humanities Lab
washu-aihumanities.blurb: An interdisciplinary lab applying computational and AI methods to literary and humanities research questions; some of its own projects study AI's behavior directly (not catalogued here) while others use AI/ML as an analysis tool for a separate humanities question (catalogued here).

biorxiv-aicopilot.label: bioRxiv — AI Co-Pilot Biotech Case Study (Northeastern University)
biorxiv-aicopilot.blurb: A Northeastern University preprint documenting a real attempt to use ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and PaperQA as research co-pilots in a biotech workflow, including an honest account of where a fully autonomous AI agent attempt failed.

linguisticdna.label: Linguistic DNA — Modelling Concepts and Semantic Change in Early Modern English
linguisticdna.blurb: An AHRC-funded project (Universities of Sheffield, Glasgow, and Sussex) applying computational distributional semantics to roughly 60,000 Early Modern English texts (EEBO-TCP, later ECCO) to track conceptual and semantic change c.1500-c.1800.
